platform: RunPod container — Linux container GPU passthrough NOT bare metal

events.observation: 65.84W GPU0 64.32W GPU1 at 0pct util 0MB VRAM entire session
events.significance: Ghost power is not VRAM-content-driven. HBM clock active with nothing to move.
Simultaneous burst both GPUs zero VRAM. HBM subsystem-level event not memory-content driven.
Only non-zero memory is static driver reservation. Not workload residue.

events.finding: HBM locked 1593MHz across idle FP32 FP16 cooldown. Active 24/7 regardless of workload.

events.pattern: SXM + HBM = ghost power. PCIe = clean.

conclusions: HBM clock 1593MHz locked 24/7 is root cause
Coordinated burst is HBM subsystem event
Cannot remediate via persistence mode or power cap — container blocked
Verda bare metal pending to confirm architectural origin

pending.goal: FP32 workload monitor VRAM fill and power during load
Poll memory.used 100Hz after process exits — does VRAM clear
Persistence mode ON — prove architectural not container artifact
First AMD vs NVIDIA ghost power comparison
